title: ophrys tommasinii subsp. litigiosa
intro: Ophrys tommasinii subsp. litigiosa, also known as the litigious bee orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that belongs to the genus Ophrys. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The litigious bee orchid is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of these conditions, providing relief from pain and discomfort. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create a remedy for its medicinal benefits.
